mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of industrial production relative to its trailing 12-month average reflect sustained manufacturing expansion and predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than zero (t-test p > 0.05) when INDPRO is above its 12-month average.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate crosses above 5.0%, the cumulative effect of monetary tightening overwhelms earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC > 0 during triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and an increase in consumer price inflation compresses profit margins and triggers risk-off repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change negative while CPIAUCSL 3-month change positive
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of joint-signal occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over main effects in a two-factor regression.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP is above its long-run trend but housing starts are contracting, the divergence between broad output and interest-rate-sensitive sectors signals an impending cyclical downturn, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 above trailing 4-quarter trend and HOUST declining over past 6 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of observations matching this regime, or the signal underperforms buy-and-hold by less than 5% annualized during identified regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month payroll employment growth signals expanding labor income and consumption trajectories, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive return accuracy falls below 55% over any 2-year window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point increase in the unemployment rate over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the mean return during triggers is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth outpaces industrial production growth, falling inventory-to-sales ratios transfer pricing power to producers, amplifying earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread between RSAFS and INDPRO growth rates fails to predict return direction with Rank IC > 0.03, or positive returns occur in < 55% of signal instances.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, accelerating consumer price inflation in the face of already elevated short-term interest rates signals policy ineffectiveness and triggers a de-rating of equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered observations, or the conditional Sharpe ratio during these episodes exceeds the unconditional Sharpe ratio.
